evals.prompt: I just deep-read 5 new files under src/backend/access/gin/ and produced one new subsystem doc knowledge/subsystems/access-gin.md. Walk me through every memory-keeping update I need to make before closing out the session.
expected_topics: append 5 rows to progress/files-examined.md (one per file, depth=deep-read, anchor commit, produced-doc link)
add one new row to progress/coverage.md (subsystems table) with name/path/last-verified-commit/confidence-summary/open-questions
edit (not append) STATE.md — bump phase if relevant, update Last activity, refresh source commit, add bullet under Done, prune Recent session logs to ~5
write a new sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>.md (today is 2026-06-02) following the 4-heading template (What I did / What I learned / What I'm unsure about / Pointers left for next time), ~50 lines
session log is append-only — never edit old ones

evals.prompt: When does a session actually warrant a sessions/ log file? I spent two hours today just exploring the planner and didn't produce any new docs. Do I log it?
expected_topics: session log written when session produced durable output: new knowledge/idioms or subsystems doc, verified-by-code fact, discovered gotcha, or a locked decision from pg-claude-plan.md §14
pure exploration with no durable output: log is optional, only if worth re-finding
trigger list explicitly in skill (wrap up, close out, sync memory files, record this gotcha, we're done for the day)
session log is the record of the act of producing, knowledge/ is the artifact — don't conflate

evals.prompt: I just discovered that a claim in knowledge/subsystems/storage-buffer.md is wrong — the BufMappingLock ordering rule I documented last week doesn't match what bufmgr.c actually does. Where do I record the correction? Do I edit the old session log that produced that doc?
expected_topics: never edit old session logs — they are append-only audit trail
write a NEW session log describing the discovery (today's date, supersedes-style)
update STATE.md to note the correction and point at the newer session log
adjust the row in progress/coverage.md (confidence-summary column for that doc)
fix the actual claim inside knowledge/subsystems/storage-buffer.md itself (the artifact)
if you re-verified the file, bump last-verified-commit on the row and append a re-verify entry to files-examined.md
